sections.source_quote: 39. The amendment of section 57— (a) by the substitution in subsection (2) for paragraph (b) of the following paragraph: '‘(b) make [the] information [prescribed by the registrar] prescribed by the Authority in joint standards made with the concurrence of the South African Reserve Bank available to the [registrar] Authority, the Prudential Authority, the South African Reserve Bank, other relevant supervisory authorities and other persons, subject to the requirements prescribed by the [registrar] Authority in joint standards made with the concurrence of the South African Reserve Bank under section 58 [regarding] as to the manner, form, and frequency of disclosure;''; and (b) by the substitution for subsection (3) of the following subsection: '‘(3) [The registrar] Joint standards may prescribe [additional] duties additional to those referred to in subsection (2) [in greater detail].''

sections.heading: The [registrar] Authority, in con-
sections.title: LiGatja ngekucocisana (Definition of LiGatja)
sections.summary: This section defines 'LiGatja' as a financial sector law that must be complied with by the Registrar Authority. It specifies that LiGatja refers to financial sector laws that are in force and applicable to the financial sector, including those that comply with directives and requests from the Registrar Authority or decisions of the appeal board.

requirements.text: LiGatja refers to financial sector laws in force and applicable to the financial sector
LiGatja must comply with directives and requests of the Registrar Authority
LiGatja must give effect to decisions of the appeal board in terms of section 105

sections.source_quote: 59. [Nobhala] LiGatja ngekucocisana neligatja lebungcwethi kufuna njalo ngemnyaka kuhlole kutsi tinsitanchanti tetimakethe— (a) tiyahambisana naloMtsetfo [kanye nemitsetfo yetinsitanchanti temakethe], i-Financial Sector Regulation Act kanye nemitsetfo yesakhiwonchanti setimakethe;

sections.heading: [Nobhala] LiGatja ngekucocisana
sections.title: LiGatja ngekucocisana (Definition of LiGatja)
sections.summary: This section defines 'LiGatja' as a financial sector law that must be complied with by the Registrar Authority. It specifies that LiGatja refers to financial sector laws that are in force and applicable to the financial sector, including those that comply with directives and requests from the Registrar Authority or decisions of the appeal board.

requirements.text: LiGatja refers to financial sector laws in force and applicable to the financial sector
LiGatja must comply with directives and requests of the Registrar Authority
LiGatja must give effect to decisions of the appeal board in terms of section 105

sections.source_quote: 59. [Nobhala] LiGatja ngekucocisana neligatja lebungcwethi kufuna njalo ngemnyaka kuhlole kutsi tinsitanchanti tetimakethe— (a) tiyahambisana naloMtsetfo [kanye nemitsetfo yetinsitanchanti temakethe], i-Financial Sector Regulation Act kanye nemitsetfo yesakhiwonchanti setimakethe;

sections.heading: The amendment of section 60—
sections.title: Amendment of section 60 to align with Registrar Authority requirements
sections.summary: This section amends section 60 by requiring that financial entities comply with directives, requests, conditions, or requirements of the Registrar Authority in terms of financial sector laws. It specifies that the Registrar Authority may cancel or suspend a licence if an entity does not comply with these requirements or decisions of the appeal board.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates that financial entities must comply with the Registrar Authority's directives and requests, which impacts data transfer and processing across borders by requiring adherence to regulatory conditions.

requirements.text: The Registrar Authority may cancel or suspend a licence if an entity does not comply with directives, requests, conditions, or requirements of the Registrar Authority
The Registrar Authority may cancel or suspend a licence if an entity does not give effect to decisions of the appeal board in terms of section 105

sections.source_quote: 42. The amendment of section 60— (a) by the substitution in subsection (1) for the words preceding paragraph (a) of the following words: 'The [registrar] Authority may, with the concurrence of the Prudential Authority and the South African Reserve Bank, cancel or suspend a licence if—';

sections.heading: The amendment of section 61—
sections.title: Amendment of section 61 to regulate market infrastructure risks
sections.summary: This section amends section 61 to prohibit market infrastructure from conducting business that would create or increase systemic risk. It requires the Registrar Authority to make determinations on such risks after consultation with the Prudential Authority and South African Reserve Bank, with specific timelines for submissions and notifications.
sections.dtia_summary: This section restricts market infrastructure from engaging in activities that increase systemic risk, which directly impacts data transfer and processing across borders by requiring risk assessments and regulatory approvals.

requirements.text: Market infrastructure may not conduct any additional business that would create or increase systemic risk
The Registrar Authority may determine requirements for market infrastructure after consultation with the Prudential Authority and South African Reserve Bank
The Registrar Authority must provide a statement of reasons for determinations within 14 days

sections.source_quote: 43. The amendment of section 61— (a) by the substitution for subsection (1) of the following subsection: ' (1) A market infrastructure may not conduct any additional business [which may introduce] if to do so would create or increase systemic risk.';

sections.heading: kuchitjiyelwa kwesigaba 63—
sections.title: Amendment of section 63 to define conversion of entities
sections.summary: This section amends section 63 to define the conversion of entities such as exchanges, central securities depositories, independent clearing houses, and central counterparties into companies. It specifies that the conversion process maintains the continuity of assets, liabilities, agreements, and legal proceedings, ensuring that rights and obligations remain binding on the new entity.

requirements.text: The conversion of entities maintains the continuity of assets, liabilities, agreements, and legal proceedings
The conversion process does not affect the terms and conditions of service for employees
The conversion process maintains the enforceability of claims and rights against the new entity

sections.source_quote: 45. kuchitjiyelwa kwesigaba 63— (a) ngekuntjintjwa kwesihloko salesigaba ngalesihloko lesilandzelako: 'Kuguculwa kwetinhlangano letilawulako, libhange lemasheya lelisemkhatsini, nendlu legunyatako letimele nome ligatja lelisemkhatsini letekuhweba';

sections.heading: The amendment of section 64 by the
sections.title: Amendment of section 64 to define asset transfer
sections.summary: This section amends section 64 to specify that assets and liabilities of amalgamating entities, including insurance and guarantees, vest in the amalgamated entity or the entity taking over such assets and liabilities, ensuring continuity of obligations to clients.
sections.dtia_summary: This section ensures that asset transfers during amalgamations maintain client obligations, which is critical for data transfer impact assessments involving financial entities.

requirements.text: Assets and liabilities of amalgamating entities vest in the amalgamated entity or the entity taking over such assets and liabilities

sections.source_quote: 46. The amendment of section 64 by the substitution in subsection (5) for paragraph (a) of the following paragraph: 'all the assets and liabilities of the amalgamating entities (or in the case of a transfer of assets and liabilities, of the entity by which the transfer is effected), including any insurance, guarantee, compensation fund or other warranty owned or maintained by any of them to cover any liabilities of clearing members of independent clearing houses or central counterparties, authorised users or participants, as the case may be, to clients, vest in and become binding upon the amalgamated entity or, as the case may be, the entity taking over such assets and liabilities or such other entity acceptable to the [registrar] Authority as the parties to the amalgamation may designate;'

sections.heading: The substitution for section 69 of the
sections.title: Substitution for section 69: Report to Registrar Authority
sections.summary: This section substitutes section 69 with a requirement for entities to report to the Registrar Authority, specifying the reporting obligations without detailed content.

requirements.text: Entities must report to the Registrar Authority

sections.source_quote: 50. The substitution for section 69 of the following section: 'Report to [registrar] Authority'

sections.heading: Within four months after the fi-
sections.title: Regulator's powers after on-site inspection
sections.summary: This section amends section 96 to grant the Financial Sector Regulation Authority powers to direct respondents to take steps or refrain from actions following on-site inspections or investigations. The Authority may require respondents to terminate or remedy irregularities disclosed during inspections, but cannot make orders under section 6D(2)(b) of the Financial Institutions (Protection of Funds) Act.
sections.dtia_summary: This section allows regulators to issue directives after inspections, which may impact data transfers if the directives involve cross-border data handling. It requires remedial actions for irregularities, potentially affecting data storage and sharing.

requirements.text: After a supervisory on-site visit or inspection or an investigation has been conducted under section 95
The [registrar] Authority may, in order to achieve the objects of this Act referred to in section 2

Direct the respondent to take any steps, or to refrain from performing or continuing to perform any act, in order to terminate or remedy any irregularity or state of affairs disclosed by the supervisory on-site visit or investigation
Provided that the registrar may not make an order contemplated in section 6D(2)(b) of the Financial Institutions (Protection of Funds) Act

sections.source_quote: 69. The amendment of section 96— (a) by the substitution for the heading of the section of the following heading: 'Powers of [registrar] Authority after supervisory on-site [visit or] inspection or investigation'; (b) by the substitution for the words preceding paragraph (a) of the following words: 'After [an] a supervisory on-site [visit or] inspection or an investigation has been conducted [under section 95], the [registrar] Authority may, in order to achieve the objects of this Act referred to in section 2—'; and (c) by the substitution for paragraph (c) of the following paragraph: '(c) direct the respondent to take any steps, or to refrain from performing or continuing to perform any act, in order to terminate or remedy any irregularity or state of affairs disclosed by the supervisory on-site [visit or] inspection or investigation: Provided that the registrar may not make an order contemplated in section 6D(2)(b) of the Financial Institutions (Protection of Funds) Act.'

sections.heading: The amendment of section 71—
sections.title: Amendment of section 71 for rule changes (alternative text)
sections.summary: This section amends section 71 to require that market infrastructure rules must not contradict regulations, conduct standards, prudential standards, or joint standards issued under this Act or the Financial Sector Regulation Act. It also outlines procedures for the Registrar Authority to amend rules, including publishing proposed amendments on the website and Gazette, and handling objections within 14 days.
sections.dtia_summary: Regulates how market infrastructure rules are amended, requiring public notice and objection periods, which affects data transfer assessments for rule compliance and transparency.

requirements.text: Market infrastructure rules may not contradict regulations, conduct standards, prudential standards, or joint standards issued under this Act or Financial Sector Regulation Act
Registrar Authority must publish proposed rule amendments on its website and Gazette within 14 days of receipt
Registrar Authority must allow interested persons to object to proposed rule amendments within 14 days of publication
Registrar Authority must publish approved rule amendments on website and Gazette with effective date

sections.source_quote: The [registrar] Authority must as soon as possible after the receipt of a proposed amendment publish— (i) the amendment on the [registrar's] Authority's website; and (ii) a notice in the Gazette that the proposed amendment is available on the [registrar's] Authority's website

sections.heading: Kuchitjiyelwa kwesigaba 71-
sections.title: Amendment of section 71 for rule changes (alternative text)
sections.summary: This section amends section 71 to require that market infrastructure rules must not contradict regulations, conduct standards, prudential standards, or joint standards issued under this Act or the Financial Sector Regulation Act. It also outlines procedures for the Registrar Authority to amend rules, including publishing proposed amendments on the website and Gazette, and handling objections within 14 days.
sections.dtia_summary: Regulates how market infrastructure rules are amended, requiring public notice and objection periods, which affects data transfer assessments for rule compliance and transparency.

requirements.text: Market infrastructure rules may not contradict regulations, conduct standards, prudential standards, or joint standards issued under this Act or Financial Sector Regulation Act
Registrar Authority must publish proposed rule amendments on its website and Gazette within 14 days of receipt
Registrar Authority must allow interested persons to object to proposed rule amendments within 14 days of publication
Registrar Authority must publish approved rule amendments on website and Gazette with effective date

sections.source_quote: The [registrar] Authority must as soon as possible after the receipt of a proposed amendment publish— (i) the amendment on the [registrar's] Authority's website; and (ii) a notice in the Gazette that the proposed amendment is available on the [registrar's] Authority's website

sections.heading: Kuchitjiyelwa kwesigaba-74—
sections.title: Amendment of section 74 for conduct standards
sections.summary: This section amends section 74 to require that conduct standards must be binding on authorized users, participants, clearing members, and their officers, employees, and clients. It specifies that conduct standards are enforceable and apply to all relevant parties in the financial sector.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires conduct standards to be binding on market participants, impacting data transfer assessments for compliance with conduct rules and client data handling.

requirements.text: Conduct standards are binding on authorized users, participants, clearing members, and their officers, employees, and clients

sections.source_quote: A [code of conduct] conduct standard is binding on authorised users, participants or clearing members of independent clearing houses or central counterparties or any other regulated person in respect of whom the [code of conduct] conduct standard was prescribed, as the case may be, and on their officers and employees and clients.

sections.heading: Kuchitjiyelwa kwesigaba 77—
sections.title: Amendment of section 77 for insider definitions
sections.summary: This section amends section 77 to define 'inside information' as information that, if made public, would likely have a material effect on the price or value of securities listed on regulated markets or derivative instruments related to such securities. It also updates the definition of 'insider' to include directors, employees, or shareholders of issuers of securities listed on regulated markets.
sections.dtia_summary: Clarifies definitions of insider and inside information, which impacts data transfer assessments for insider trading and sensitive financial data handling.

requirements.text: Inside information is information that, if made public, would likely have a material effect on the price or value of securities listed on regulated markets or derivative instruments
Insider includes directors, employees, or shareholders of issuers of securities listed on regulated markets

sections.source_quote: if it were made public, would be likely to have a material effect on the price or value of any security listed on a regulated market or of any derivative instrument related to such a security

sections.heading: Kuchitjiyelwa kwesigaba 78—
sections.title: Amendment of section 78 for insider offenses
sections.summary: This section amends section 78 to define insider offenses, including the requirement that insiders must prove on a balance of probabilities that they disclosed inside information necessary for their employment functions, unrelated to securities trading, to avoid criminal liability.
sections.dtia_summary: Defines conditions under which insiders may avoid liability for disclosing inside information, impacting data transfer assessments for insider trading and sensitive financial data handling.

requirements.text: Insiders are not guilty of offenses if they prove on a balance of probabilities that disclosure was necessary for proper performance of employment functions unrelated to securities trading
Insiders who encourage or cause others to trade based on inside information commit an offense

sections.source_quote: An insider who knows that he or she has inside information and who encourages or causes another person to deal or discourages or stops another person from dealing in the securities listed on a regulated market or in derivative instruments related to such securities, to which the inside information relates or which are likely to be affected by it, commits an offence.

sections.heading: The amendment of section 82—
sections.title: Amendment of section 82 to specify claim submission procedures
sections.summary: This section amends section 82 by substituting the claim submission process to require that claimants submit claims within 90 days of a notice published in a national newspaper or on the Authority's website. It also specifies that claimants must prove their claims to the Authority's satisfaction.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates specific timelines and procedures for claim submissions, which could impact data transfer processes involving financial claims and dispute resolution mechanisms.

requirements.text: Submit claims to the Authority within 90 days from the date of publication of a notice in one national newspaper or on the Authority's website
Prove to the reasonable satisfaction of the Authority that claims are valid

sections.source_quote: 58. kuchitjiyelwa kwesigaba 82— (a) ngekuntjintjwa kwekuvetwa kwe 'likomidi lelibeka imitsetfo' ngeligama lelitsi 'liGatja' nobe'; (b) ngekuntjintjwa kwesigatjana (4) ngalesigatjana lesilandzelako: '(4) Noma ngabe nguliphi linani lelibekwe [yibhodi] liGatja njengemphumela walenchubo'

sections.heading: kuchitjiyelwa kwesigaba 82—
sections.title: Amendment of section 82 to specify claim submission procedures
sections.summary: This section amends section 82 by substituting the claim submission process to require that claimants submit claims within 90 days of a notice published in a national newspaper or on the Authority's website. It also specifies that claimants must prove their claims to the Authority's satisfaction.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates specific timelines and procedures for claim submissions, which could impact data transfer processes involving financial claims and dispute resolution mechanisms.

requirements.text: Submit claims to the Authority within 90 days from the date of publication of a notice in one national newspaper or on the Authority's website
Prove to the reasonable satisfaction of the Authority that claims are valid

sections.heading: The Authority may—
sections.title: Authority may establish conduct standards for market abuse monitoring
sections.summary: This section grants the Authority the power to establish conduct standards or regulator's directives for monitoring and identifying possible contraventions of the Financial Sector Regulation Act. It also allows the Authority to create conduct standards for the disclosure of inside information.
sections.dtia_summary: This section enables the Authority to set standards for market abuse monitoring and inside information disclosure, which could influence data transfer practices related to financial market activities.

requirements.text: After consultation with relevant regulated markets, the Authority may make conduct standards for monitoring and identifying possible contraventions
The Authority may make conduct standards for the disclosure of inside information

sections.source_quote: 84. The Authority may— (a) after consultation with the relevant regulated markets in the Republic,— (i) make conduct standards, or (ii) give regulator's directives for the implementation of such systems as are necessary for the effective monitoring and identification of possible contraventions of this Chapter; and (b) make conduct standards for the disclosure of inside information.''

sections.heading: (1) (a) The Directorate established
sections.title: Directorate of Market Abuse established under Financial Sector Regulation Act
sections.summary: This section establishes the Directorate of Market Abuse as the continuation of the Insider Trading Directorate from previous legislation. It specifies that the Authority may determine the functions, powers, and duties of the Directorate, including conducting investigations into offences under specified sections of the Act. The Directorate consists of members and alternate members appointed by the Authority, with specific industry representation requirements.
sections.dtia_summary: This section creates a regulatory body for market abuse oversight, which impacts data transfer practices through its authority to investigate financial misconduct and manage related data flows.

requirements.text: The Directorate of Market Abuse continues to exist under the name Directorate of Market Abuse despite the repeal of previous Acts

The Authority may determine the functions, powers, and duties of the Directorate, including conducting investigations into offences under sections 78, 80, 81, and section 135(2) of the Financial Sector Regulation Act
The Directorate consists of members and alternate members appointed by the Authority
Members holding office at the date Part 6 of Chapter 17 comes into force remain as members for their terms and conditions
Members and alternate members must have appropriate knowledge of financial markets
Members and alternate members must disclose any interest in matters being considered by the Directorate

sections.heading: (1) (a) iBhodi yeBacondzisi
sections.title: Directorate of Market Abuse established under Financial Sector Regulation Act
sections.summary: This section establishes the Directorate of Market Abuse as the continuation of the Insider Trading Directorate from previous legislation. It specifies that the Authority may determine the functions, powers, and duties of the Directorate, including conducting investigations into offences under specified sections of the Act. The Directorate consists of members and alternate members appointed by the Authority, with specific industry representation requirements.
sections.dtia_summary: This section creates a regulatory body for market abuse oversight, which impacts data transfer practices through its authority to investigate financial misconduct and manage related data flows.

requirements.text: The Directorate of Market Abuse continues to exist under the name Directorate of Market Abuse despite the repeal of previous Acts

The Authority may determine the functions, powers, and duties of the Directorate, including conducting investigations into offences under sections 78, 80, 81, and section 135(2) of the Financial Sector Regulation Act
The Directorate consists of members and alternate members appointed by the Authority
Members holding office at the date Part 6 of Chapter 17 comes into force remain as members for their terms and conditions
Members and alternate members must have appropriate knowledge of financial markets
Members and alternate members must disclose any interest in matters being considered by the Directorate

sections.heading: The substitution for section 88 of the
sections.title: Substitution for section 88 on confidentiality and information sharing
sections.summary: This section replaces section 88 with a new provision on confidentiality and sharing of information. It specifies that information must be handled confidentially and shared only as necessary for legitimate purposes.
sections.dtia_summary: This section governs the confidentiality and sharing of information, which directly impacts data transfer practices and compliance with privacy regulations.

requirements.text: Information must be handled confidentially and shared only as necessary for legitimate purposes

sections.source_quote: 63. The substitution for section 88 of the following section: 'Confidentiality and sharing of information'

sections.heading: The [directorate] Authority may
sections.title: Authority sharing financial information with regulators
sections.summary: The Financial Sector Regulation Authority may share information with specified entities including the Takeover Regulation Panel, South African Reserve Bank, Prudential Authority, Financial Intelligence Centre, National Treasury, and persons responsible for insider trading regulation. This applies to matters dealt with under the Financial Sector Regulation Act. The sharing is permitted without specific conditions but requires the information to relate to matters covered by the Act.
sections.dtia_summary: This section allows the regulator to share financial information with other authorities, which may impact cross-border data transfers if the recipient entities operate internationally. It requires data sharing with specific regulatory bodies but does not impose direct data transfer restrictions.

requirements.text: The [directorate] Authority may share information concerning any matter dealt with in terms of this Chapter
With the [institutions which have nominated persons to the directorate, the] Takeover Regulation Panel
Established by section 196 of the Companies Act
The South African Reserve Bank
The Prudential Authority
The Independent Regulatory Board for Auditors constituted in terms of the Auditing Profession Act
A licensed exchange, a licensed central securities depository, or a licensed independent clearing house market infrastructure
The Financial Intelligence Centre established by the Financial Intelligence Centre Act
The National Treasury
The Minister
Persons inside the Republic or elsewhere responsible for regulating, investigating or prosecuting insider trading
Prohibited trading practices and other market abuses

sections.source_quote: 88. The [directorate] Authority may share information concerning any matter dealt with in terms of this Chapter with the [institutions which have nominated persons to the directorate, the] Takeover Regulation Panel, established by section 196 of the Companies Act, the South African Reserve Bank, the Prudential Authority, the Independent Regulatory Board for Auditors constituted in terms of the Auditing Profession Act, a licensed exchange, a licensed central securities depository, or a licensed independent clearing house market infrastructure, the Financial Intelligence Centre established by the Financial Intelligence Centre Act, the National Treasury, the Minister and the persons, inside the Republic or elsewhere, responsible for regulating, investigating or prosecuting insider trading, prohibited trading practices and other market abuses.''

sections.heading: The amendment of section 90 by the
sections.title: Accounting records and financial statements requirements
sections.summary: This section amends section 90 to require that regulated entities maintain accounting records and prepare annual financial statements that conform with financial reporting standards prescribed under the Companies Act. The records must be audited by an auditor appointed under section 89 within a period determined by the registrar or as allowed by the registrar on application.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates financial reporting standards that could impact data transfers involving financial records. It requires audited financial statements, which may affect data storage and sharing practices across borders.

requirements.text: Maintain on a continual basis the accounting records prescribed by the registrar determined in joint standards
Prepare annual financial statements that conform with the financial reporting standards prescribed under the Companies Act
Contain the information that may be prescribed by the registrar determined in joint standards
Cause such accounting records and annual financial statements to be audited by an auditor appointed under section 89
Within a period prescribed by the registrar determined in joint standards
Or such later date as the registrar may allow on application by a regulated person

sections.source_quote: 64. The amendment of section 90 by the substitution for paragraphs (a) and (b) of the following paragraphs: '(a) maintain on a continual basis the accounting records [prescribed by the registrar] determined in joint standards and prepare annual financial statements that conform with the financial reporting standards prescribed under the Companies Act and contain the information that may be [prescribed by the registrar] determined in joint standards; (b) cause such accounting records and annual financial statements to be audited by an auditor appointed under section 89, within a period [prescribed by the registrar] determined in joint standards or such later date as the [registrar] Authority may allow on application by a regulated person;'

sections.heading: (1) If the [registrar] Authority
sections.title: Investigation powers for financial misconduct
sections.summary: The Financial Sector Regulation Authority may investigate complaints or allegations of misconduct by securities service providers. If the Authority has reason to believe a contravention is occurring, it may direct the respondent to provide information, documents, or records reasonably required by the Authority. The Authority may also require the respondent to appear before it at a specified time and place.
sections.dtia_summary: This section grants the regulator authority to investigate financial misconduct, which may impact data transfers if the investigation involves cross-border data collection or sharing. It requires detailed records from respondents, potentially affecting data storage and sharing practices.

requirements.text: If the [registrar] Authority receives a complaint, charge or allegation that a person who provides securities services is contravening or failing to comply with any provision of this Act
Or if the [registrar] Authority has reason to believe that such a contravention or failure is taking place

The [registrar] Authority may investigate the matter by directing that respondent in writing to provide the registrar with any information, document or record reasonably required by the registrar about such services
The [registrar] Authority may direct the respondent to appear before the registrar at a specified time and place

sections.heading: The amendment of section 96—
sections.title: Regulator's powers after on-site inspection
sections.summary: This section amends section 96 to grant the Financial Sector Regulation Authority powers to direct respondents to take steps or refrain from actions following on-site inspections or investigations. The Authority may require respondents to terminate or remedy irregularities disclosed during inspections, but cannot make orders under section 6D(2)(b) of the Financial Institutions (Protection of Funds) Act.
sections.dtia_summary: This section allows regulators to issue directives after inspections, which may impact data transfers if the directives involve cross-border data handling. It requires remedial actions for irregularities, potentially affecting data storage and sharing.

requirements.text: After a supervisory on-site visit or inspection or an investigation has been conducted under section 95
The [registrar] Authority may, in order to achieve the objects of this Act referred to in section 2

Direct the respondent to take any steps, or to refrain from performing or continuing to perform any act, in order to terminate or remedy any irregularity or state of affairs disclosed by the supervisory on-site visit or investigation
Provided that the registrar may not make an order contemplated in section 6D(2)(b) of the Financial Institutions (Protection of Funds) Act

sections.source_quote: 69. The amendment of section 96— (a) by the substitution for the heading of the section of the following heading: 'Powers of [registrar] Authority after supervisory on-site [visit or] inspection or investigation'; (b) by the substitution for the words preceding paragraph (a) of the following words: 'After [an] a supervisory on-site [visit or] inspection or an investigation has been conducted [under section 95], the [registrar] Authority may, in order to achieve the objects of this Act referred to in section 2—'; and (c) by the substitution for paragraph (c) of the following paragraph: '(c) direct the respondent to take any steps, or to refrain from performing or continuing to perform any act, in order to terminate or remedy any irregularity or state of affairs disclosed by the supervisory on-site [visit or] inspection or investigation: Provided that the registrar may not make an order contemplated in section 6D(2)(b) of the Financial Institutions (Protection of Funds) Act.'

sections.heading: The amendment of section 105—
sections.title: Appeals against regulatory decisions
sections.summary: This section amends section 105 to allow persons aggrieved by decisions of the Financial Sector Regulation Authority or exchanges to appeal. The appeal process covers decisions related to authorizations, listings of securities, and participation in central securities depositories. The appeal mechanism is designed to address disputes with regulatory bodies.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes an appeal process for regulatory decisions, which may impact data transfers if the decisions involve cross-border financial entities. It provides a mechanism for resolving disputes over data handling practices.

requirements.text: A person aggrieved by a decision of the [registrar] Authority under a power conferred or a duty imposed upon the [registrar] Authority by or under this Act or the Financial Sector Regulation Act
An exchange to refuse an application by that person to be admitted as an authorised user
An exchange to withdraw the authorisation of an authorised user or to direct an authorised user to terminate the access to the exchange by an officer or employee of such authorised user
An exchange to defer, refuse or grant an application for the inclusion of securities in the list or to remove securities from the list or to suspend the trading in listed securities
A central securities depository to refuse an application by a person to be accepted as a participant

A central securities depository to terminate the participation of a participant or to direct a participant to terminate the access to the central securities depository by an officer or employee of a participant

sections.source_quote: 74. The amendment of section 105— (a) by the substitution for subsection (1) of the following subsection: '(1) A person aggrieved by a decision of— (a) the [registrar] Authority under a power conferred or a duty imposed upon the [registrar] Authority by or under this Act or the Financial Sector Regulation Act; (b) an exchange to refuse an application by that person to be admitted as an authorised user; (c) an exchange to withdraw the authorisation of an authorised user or to direct an authorised user to terminate the access to the exchange by an officer or employee of such authorised user; (d) an exchange to defer, refuse or grant an application for the inclusion of securities in the list or to remove securities from the list or to suspend the trading in listed securities; (e) a central securities depository to refuse an application by a person to be accepted as a participant; (f) a central securities depository to terminate the participation of a participant or to direct a participant to terminate the access to the central securities depository by an officer or employee of a participant;'

sections.heading: The amendment of section 108 by
sections.title: Amendment of fee determination authority in financial regulations
sections.summary: This section amends section 108 by substituting a subsection that allows the Authority to determine fees for matters under the Act. It specifies who must pay fees, the payment method, and interest for overdue fees. The Authority has discretion to set these fees within the framework of the Act.
sections.dtia_summary: This amendment clarifies fee determination mechanisms but does not directly impact data transfer assessments as it pertains to fee structures rather than data flows.

requirements.text: The Authority may determine fees in respect of matters contemplated in this Act
The person by whom the fee must be paid is specified
The manner of payment of fees is specified
Interest payable in respect of overdue fees is specified

sections.source_quote: The Authority may determine fees in respect of matters contemplated in this Act and, in relation to those fees, the person by whom the fee must be paid, the manner of payment thereof and, where necessary, the interest payable in respect of overdue fees.

sections.heading: The amendment of section 110—
sections.title: Clearing house licensing requirements for central counterparties
sections.summary: This section amends section 110 by deleting subsection (5) and adding a new subsection (6). It mandates that clearing houses performing central counterparty functions must be licensed by the South African Reserve Bank and Prudential Authority by January 2022. The licensing process requires approval from the Authority, South African Reserve Bank, and Prudential Authority.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes licensing requirements for clearing houses, which may impact data transfer assessments if they handle financial data across borders.
sections.authority: South African Reserve Bank, Prudential Authority

requirements.text: Clearing houses must be licensed as either an associated or independent clearing house by 31 December 2021
Clearing houses must be approved by the Authority, South African Reserve Bank, and Prudential Authority
Clearing houses must be licensed as both independent and central counterparty effective 1 January 2022

sections.source_quote: a clearing house performing the functions of a central counterparty must comply with any requirements imposed by regulations or standards, and must— until 31 December 2021, be licensed as either an associated clearing house or an independent clearing house, and be approved by the Authority, the South African Reserve Bank and the Prudential Authority

sections.heading: The amendment of the arrangement
sections.title: Amendments to financial sector regulation arrangements
sections.summary: This section amends multiple sections of the Act by inserting new items, substituting headings, and modifying existing items. It introduces a relationship between the Act and Financial Sector Regulation Act, criteria for external market infrastructure recognition, and licensing requirements for clearing houses and central counterparties.
sections.dtia_summary: This section restructures the regulatory framework for financial entities, which may affect data transfer assessments through licensing and recognition requirements for financial infrastructure.

Licensing of clearing house and central counterparty is defined

sections.heading: The amendment of section 1—
sections.title: Definition of Authority in financial regulations
sections.summary: This section amends section 1 by inserting definitions for 'Authority', 'conduct standard', 'Register', and 'Tribunal', while deleting other definitions. It specifies that the Authority refers to the Financial Sector Conduct Authority established under the Financial Sector Regulation Act.
sections.dtia_summary: This section clarifies the regulatory authority responsible for financial sector conduct, which may impact data transfer assessments through jurisdictional clarity.

requirements.text: Authority means the Financial Sector Conduct Authority established in terms of section 56 of the Financial Sector Regulation Act

sections.source_quote: ‘Authority' means the Financial Sector Conduct Authority established in terms of section 56 of the Financial Sector Regulation Act

sections.heading: The insertion after section 1 of the
sections.title: Financial Sector Regulation Act alignment with other laws
sections.summary: This section mandates that the Financial Sector Regulation Act aligns with the Financial Sector Regulation Act. It requires that any provision of the Act that refers to an inspection must be interpreted as an investigation under the Financial Sector Regulation Act. It also specifies that references to the Authority publishing information online must be interpreted as publishing in the Register, and that prescribed fees must be determined under section 237 of the Financial Sector Regulation Act.
sections.dtia_summary: This section clarifies how the Financial Sector Regulation Act interacts with other regulatory frameworks, particularly regarding inspections, publications, and fees. It ensures consistency in regulatory procedures across different legal instruments.

requirements.text: A reference in this Act to an inspection must be read as a reference to an investigation in terms of the Financial Sector Regulation Act
A reference in this Act to the Authority publishing information or a document on a web site must be read as a reference to the Authority publishing the information or document in the Register
The Authority may also publish the information or document on its web site
A reference in this Act to a prescribed fee must be read as a reference to the relevant fee determined in terms of section 237 and Chapter 16 of the Financial Sector Regulation Act
A reference in this Act to a review of a decision of the Authority must be read as a reference to a reconsideration of the decision by the Tribunal in terms of the Financial Sector Regulation Act

sections.source_quote: A reference in this Act to an inspection in terms of a provision of this Act must be read as a reference to an investigation in terms of the Financial Sector Regulation Act.

sections.heading: Amendment of the arrangement of
sections.title: Amendment of the arrangement of sections
sections.summary: This section amends the arrangement of sections by inserting items 1A and 1B, which define the relationship between the Financial Sector Regulation Act and the Financial Sector Regulation Act, and regulatory instruments. It specifies that any matter prescribed by the Authority in respect of which notice in the Gazette is specifically required by this Act is considered a regulatory instrument.
sections.dtia_summary: This section updates the section numbering and definitions to clarify regulatory instruments and the relationship between the Financial Sector Regulation Act and other regulatory frameworks, which is relevant for understanding how regulatory compliance is structured.
